assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following are true of Delacroix's *Liberty Leading the People*?**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3197fc29bf37a0df193458e6db75e789.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**All of the above are true.**

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/becoming-modern/romanticism/romanticism-in-france/v/delacroix-liberty-leading-the-people-1830)**

choices.content: It depicts a scene at a street barricade on the cobblestone streets of Paris during the French Revolution of 1830.
Delacroix captured realistic details of the street violence.
The representation of Liberty draws on the classical tradition in the profile pose but also appears more real, less ideal than classical figures.
The revolution of 1830 ushered in a constitutional monarchy, but still only a small percentage of the population was eligible to vote.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The woman is an allegorical figure of Liberty who holds the tricolor flag symbolizing the Revolution's values of . . .**
 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2223f8c14f55984716e47febf6be739e.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**The woman is an allegorical figure of Liberty who holds the tricolor flag symbolizing the Revolution's values of liberty, equality, and fraternity. **

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/becoming-modern/romanticism/romanticism-in-france/v/delacroix-liberty-leading-the-people-1830)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**The painting was carefully composed to include only upper-class men who joined together to overthrow the monarchy.**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3197fc29bf37a0df193458e6db75e789.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**The painting is carefully composed to include the various types of men from different social classes who joined together to overthrow the monarchy.**

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/becoming-modern/romanticism/romanticism-in-france/v/delacroix-liberty-leading-the-people-1830)**

assessment_items.question_content:
The painting depicts the dead and wounded from both sides of the fighting.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Both revolutionaries and French forces loyal to the king are depicted.**

[Watch this video to review](/humanities/becoming-modern/romanticism/romanticism-in-france/v/delacroix-liberty-leading-the-people-1830)
